risks: No zone redundancy or multi-region failover configured for any resource; single-region deployment in eastus creates a single point of failure
No private endpoints or network isolation for IoT Hub or Cosmos DB, leaving resources accessible over the public internet
No managed identity configured; resources may default to key-based authentication, increasing credential exposure risk
No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Log Analytics, Application Insights) included in the plan
Stream Analytics compatibilityLevel is 1.0; version 1.2 is current and recommended for latest features and fixes
Logic App references a Twilio API connection via $connections parameter but no API connection resource is defined in the plan
correctionsRecommended: Enable zone redundancy on Cosmos DB by setting isZoneRedundant to true in the locations array
Add private endpoints for IoT Hub and Cosmos DB to restrict public network access
Configure managed identity on Stream Analytics and Logic App for secure, keyless authentication to IoT Hub and Cosmos DB
Add a Log Analytics workspace resource and configure diagnostic settings on all resources for monitoring and alerting
Upgrade Stream Analytics compatibilityLevel from 1.0 to 1.2
Define the Twilio API connection as an explicit Microsoft.Web/connections resource, or replace with Azure Communication Services for native SMS
Add resource tags across all resources for cost tracking and operational management
Disable key-based access on Cosmos DB and use Azure RBAC for data-plane access
